scope_boundary: Public-safe local external Circle cyclic-mixer receipt import only. The slice records one pinned `CC-AI-CONTRACT-MIXER-001` structural/accounting fixture at Circle commit `63b0f511`, dense-reference parity with `max_abs_dense_delta=0`, block-cyclic parameter accounting with `block_to_dense_ratio=0.0625`, seven required theorem IDs, two planner recommendations, contract fingerprint `b3e3e0cf420d9e8e79a28a55ef8322f9a214c8d5a957dd8b06e5e5373c684ea5`, command-output digests, targeted Circle CLI test output `3 passed in 2.49s`, and targeted contract-ready recommendation test output `1 passed in 1.47s`. It does not prove cyclic-mixer model quality, speed, memory scaling, hardware efficiency, training stability, deployment readiness, transfer, benchmark performance, ASI, or CoilRA chapter-core promotion.
transition_reason: The cyclic-mixer receipt slice makes one Circle circulant/block-cyclic structural fixture concrete in the book and closes one stale 'no cyclic mixer receipt import' blocker for the CoilRA chapter. The evidence remains an external-project receipt import with no ASI-side cyclic-mixer benchmark, no ordinary dense, LoRA, RoPE, learned, recurrent, or state-space baselines with workload metrics, no hardware-kernel measurement, no model-quality result, and no independent review, so it blocks promotion rather than moving support state.
